sources.notes: Read the friendship graph, local MEX condition, output requirement, and the constraints sum(n) <= 2e5.
The official editorial gives the alternating 0/1 construction rooted at x and the exact parity condition for replacing a_x by 2.
Independently checked every affected neighborhood and exhaustively verified the construction for every x<y and every 3<=n<=14.

annotation.summary: Label the vertices of a cycle plus the extra edge (x,y) so that every vertex label equals the MEX of the labels on its neighbors.
annotation.constraints: Up to 1e4 tests; 3 <= n <= 2e5, 1 <= x < y <= n, and sum n <= 2e5. Any nonnegative labels up to 1e9 are allowed, so a linear explicit construction is intended.
annotation.core_idea: Starting at x and walking around the cycle, assign 0,1,0,1,... . An even cycle whose chord endpoints have opposite parity is already valid: every 0 sees only 1 values and every 1 sees only 0 values. Otherwise, either the odd cycle closes with equal endpoint parity or the chord joins equal parity classes. Change only a_x from 0 to 2. In the odd-cycle case, x then sees both 0 and 1 on its two cycle sides; in the even same-parity-chord case, x sees 0 through y and 1 through its cycle neighbors. The vertices adjacent to x retain their missing value because their other cycle neighbor supplies the needed 0 or 1, and y is not disturbed by adding a neighbor labeled 2. Thus the repair is needed exactly when n is odd or x-y is even.
annotation.complexity: O(n) time and O(n) output storage per test case
tricks: Anchor the alternating pattern at x so chord behavior is determined by parity of x-y.
Use label 2 as a one-vertex repair for either an odd cycle or a same-parity chord.
Verify only x, y, and the two cycle neighbors of x after the repair; all other local MEX neighborhoods are unchanged.

solution_variants.name: alternating-cycle-with-one-two
solution_variants.summary: Write alternating 0 and 1 while traversing from x; set a_x=2 iff n is odd or x-y is even, otherwise leave the pure alternation.
solution_variants.complexity: O(n) time and O(n) memory for the answer

tags.evidence: The required output is obtained by an explicit alternating labeling plus at most one deterministic repair, with no search or optimization.
Correctness is exactly the local identities mex({1})=0, mex({0})=1, and mex({0,1})=2 for each neighborhood.
Whether pure alternation works is determined by parity of n and parity of the distance x-y; these conditions decide if the label-2 repair is necessary.
The 0/1 base assignment is the two-coloring of the cycle; an odd cycle or a chord within one parity class is precisely the obstruction repaired with color 2.
The proof separates the already-bipartite parity case, odd n, and even n with a same-parity chord, then checks the few affected vertices.
Implementation only needs circular indexing and careful parity after converting x and y to zero-based indices.
